description: End-to-end execution smoke fixture (issue #90, extended by #93): a tiny 2-statement program opts into execution and asserts the generic `instruction` start event emitted before each top-level statement, plus the minimal `print` event issue #93 adds carrying the evaluated argument's value, proving parse -> execute -> evaluate -> event-capture -> compare works. Full `print` semantics (multiple operands, formatting, newlines) land with issue #98, extending the `print` event this fixture already exercises.
